sections.paragraph:
καταψεύδομαι aor. 3 sg. κατεψεύσατο (TestAbr B; Ath.) (s. ψεύδομαι; Eur. et al.; OGI 8, 14f; PFlor 382, 57; Wsd 1:11; Philo; Tat. 3, 2; Ath. 35, 1) tell lies (against) τινός in contradiction or in opposition to someone (Hyperid. 3, 18 τ. θεοῦ; Dio Chrys. 6, 17 τῆς θεοῦ; Chariton 5, 6, 10; Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. 5, 37 p. 198, 10; Sextus 367; En 104:9; TestAbr B 10 p. 114, 19 [Stone p. 76]; Jos., Bell. 6, 288) ITr 10. τινός w. ptc. foll. say falsely about someone that Dg 4:3. τινός τι charge someone falsely w. someth. (Pla., Phd. 85a, Euthyd. 283f, Rep. 2, 381d; 3, 391d al.; Philo, Op. M. 7 τοῦ θεοῦ ἀπραξίαν κ.) Hv 1, 1, 7.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Hyperid Hyperid , IV b.c.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Sextus Sextus , end of II a.d.—List 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

ptc. ptc. = participle

foll. foll. = followed, following

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

someth. someth. = something

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Hv Hv = Visions

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 530
